reasoning: The market asks whether the U.S. will commence a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Iran between June 18 and December 31, 2025. It is now April 2026. Evidence from multiple credible sources (e.g., Al Jazeera, BBC, Brookings Institution, ACLED) reveals that while a short, 12-day war did occur between the U.S., Israel, and Iran in late June and early July 2025 (involving major airstrikes on Iranian nuclear and military facilities), the U.S. did not launch a ground invasion or any operation "intended to establish control" over Iranian territory in 2025. Reports published in early to mid-2026 clearly state that the U.S. had not authorized any ground operations in Iran as of March 2026 and was only potentially considering such options (like seizing islands) in 2026. Therefore, no qualifying U.S. offensive intended to establish territorial control over Iran occurred in 2025. This allows me to confidently assign my lowest possible probability to "Yes."
